test.notes: Pre-existing environmental test pollution in swell-core::config::tests::test_higher_layer_overrides_lower_layer (assertion `left == right` failed: left: 50, right: 20). This is the same multi-threaded contamination issue documented in round 2. Passes with --test-threads=1. A new test failure was introduced by fix commit d65b7fd in test_daemon_status_returns_health_info (assertion `mcp_health.is_empty()` failed) - FIXED by updating the test assertion to expect the placeholder entry. Final swell-daemon tests all pass (84 tests).

issues.description: MCP health still returns placeholder data, not 'actual server health' as required by VAL-API-002. Fix commit d65b7fd improved from empty HashMap to HashMap with entry '_status' -> 'pending - MCP manager not yet wired into daemon'. While this descriptive placeholder is better than an empty map, it still does not satisfy VAL-API-002's explicit requirement for 'per-server MCP health statuses reflect actual server health'. The architectural constraint (swell-daemon cannot access MCP manager in swell-tools) remains unresolved.
issues.requiredFix: One of three options: (1) Add MCP health monitoring infrastructure to swell-daemon that can track MCP server status, (2) Adjust VAL-API-002 contract to clarify that MCP health is informational placeholder when MCP manager is unavailable, (3) Have swell-daemon hold a reference to MCP manager to query live status. This is an architectural cross-crate issue - swell-daemon intentionally does not have access to MCP manager which lives in swell-tools.

nonBlockingIssues.description: Test assertion at commands.rs:2152 expected empty mcp_health but fix commit added placeholder entry. Fixed by updating assertion to check for '_status' key with 'pending' value. Test now passes.
nonBlockingIssues.fixApplied: Updated test assertion in commands.rs:2152 to expect mcp_health.contains_key('_status') with placeholder value instead of empty HashMap.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance on VAL-API-002 / VAL-MCP-* architectural boundaries: swell-daemon intentionally does not access MCP manager (in swell-tools). When features require MCP health monitoring from the daemon, the architectural decision must be: (a) grant daemon access to MCP status via shared state, (b) create separate MCP health tracking mechanism accessible to daemon, or (c) adjust the API contract to reflect placeholder-only behavior when MCP manager is unavailable.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: BLOCK-1: MCP health placeholder in commands.rs:329-333 documents swell-daemon lacks MCP manager access. VAL-API-002 requires 'per-server MCP health statuses reflect actual server health' which cannot be satisfied without architectural changes.

previousRound.summary: Round 2 found daemon-status with 1 blocking issue (BLOCK-1: MCP health hardcoded empty HashMap). Fix commit 5b2de0f addressed BLOCK-2 and BLOCK-3. Round 3 addresses new fix commit d65b7fd which improved MCP health from empty HashMap to descriptive placeholder.

fixCommitAnalysis.message: fix(daemon-status): add MCP health tracking placeholder to DaemonStatus
changes: Changed mcp_health from empty HashMap to HashMap with entry '_status' -> 'pending - MCP manager not yet wired into daemon'
Improved comment to clarify architectural constraint
fixedIssues: test_daemon_status_returns_health_info assertion failure (fixed in this round)
remainingIssues: BLOCK-1: MCP health placeholder is not 'actual server health' per VAL-API-002
